ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product; this record is a fast-food feast set (burger + fries + drink + pie) so the match is approximate at the burger component level. 'closest_parent' is used as the feast is a composite of items, with the burger being the primary named component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hamburger; the feast set includes fries, beverage, and pie components not captured by this single code. Composite meal code not available; burger component used as primary anchor. 'closest_parent' is used as the feast is a composite of items, with the burger being the primary named component.

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + medium fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard feast set comprising a spicy tomato beef burger, medium fries, a regular drink, and a pie, weighing approximately 680 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Beef Burger Feast — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Beef Burger Feast (cr_id G00010118). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific fast-food feast set. GI of 72 is estimated using the white-bread-bun burger composite analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database (hamburger on white bun GI ~66–72; refined white bun GI ~71). The feast set includes deep-fried fries (GI ~75) and a likely sugary beverage (GI ~63 for cola), which collectively support a blended GI estimate of approximately 72 (high). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (72 × 200.7 g carbohydrate in 680 g serving) / 100 ≈ 144.6. Carbohydrate per serving = 29.57 g/100g × 680 g = 200.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 487 mg/100 g; full feast set delivers approximately 3,312 mg sodium per serving, far exceeding the 2,000 mg/day WHO recommended limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this feast set or, at minimum, substitute the sugary beverage with water, request reduced sauce, and avoid adding salt to fries, as the combined sodium load from burger, fries, and sauces poses a significant blood-pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) and very high glycemic load per serving (~144.6) driven by refined white bun, deep-fried fries, and a likely sugary beverage; dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g/100 g.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this feast set due to its very high glycemic load; if consumed, the sugary beverage must be replaced with water or unsweetened tea, fries should be declined, and portion size should be strictly limited to reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Trans fat present at 0.3 g/100 g (approximately 2 g per feast serving), saturated fat ~3.5 g/100 g, cholesterol 29 mg/100 g, and sodium 487 mg/100 g collectively elevate LDL, promote endothelial dysfunction, and raise blood pressure.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, high sodium, and deep-fried components creates a strongly atherogenic and hypertensive dietary load that is inconsistent with heart-healthy eating guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beef (arachidonic acid precursor), trans fat 0.3 g/100 g, nightshade-based tomato sauce, sodium >400 mg/100 g, and deep-fried fries generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) collectively create a strongly pro-inflammatory meal.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this feast set as the combination of beef, trans fat, nightshade sauce, and AGE-rich deep-fried fries is strongly pro-inflammatory and may exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Beef Burger Feast is a fast-food combo meal sold in Singapore, comprising a spicy tomato beef burger (grilled beef patty on a refined white bun with spicy tomato sauce and salad garnish), a serving of medium deep-fried french fries, a regular carbonated or juice beverage, and a pie. The meal is ultra-processed and calorie-dense, delivering approximately 263 kcal per 100 g and roughly 1,788 kcal for the full feast set of approximately 680 g. The burger bun is made from refined wheat flour, contributing a high glycemic index of approximately 72 and a very high glycemic load per serving of approximately 144.6. Protein content is moderate at 13.3 g/100 g, primarily from the beef patty. Fat is 10.18 g/100 g with trans fat present at approximately 0.3 g/100 g from the deep-frying process. Sodium is high at 487 mg/100 g, translating to approximately 3,312 mg per full feast serving — well above the WHO single-meal sodium guidance. Dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g/100 g, providing minimal glycaemic buffering. The meal is suitable for occasional consumption by healthy adults but is clinically contraindicated for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, high glycemic load, trans fat content, and pro-inflammatory ingredient profile. It is widely available at fast-food outlets across Singapore and is classified under Western cuisine adapted for the Singaporean market.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010118) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database (hamburger on white bun) as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific feast set; this introduces moderate uncertainty for the GI component only, but all other nutrient values are exact from the approved primary source.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this feast set as of 2026-04-16. 2. trans_fat_g set to 0.3 based on the input health_suitability caution noting 'Trans fat 0.3g/100g'; this value is consistent with deep-fried fast-food products but is not independently confirmed in the HPB per_100g table — flagged for verification. 3. saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.5 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central analogous fast-food beef burger data (USDA SR Legacy item 'Fast foods, hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables') as the HPB record does not report saturated fat separately; this is an approximation. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this feast set as of 2026-04-16. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (hamburger on white bun GI range 66–72); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 6. Serving size of 680 g is an estimate based on typical fast-food feast set weights (burger ~220 g + fries ~120 g + drink ~300 g + pie ~40 g); actual weight may vary by outlet. 7. is_halal set to true based on the food being sold in Singapore fast-food context where halal certification is standard for beef burger products; this should be verified against the specific outlet's halal certification. 8. All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrates_g) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
